perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall not disclose any Conf idential Information to any third parties for three (3) years following the date of its disclo sure by Disclosing Part y to Receiv ing Party, except to Receiving Party's consultants as set out below. However, Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information in accordance with judicial or other governmental order, provided that the Receiving Part y shall give the Disclosing Part y reasonable notice prior to such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall not disclose any Conf idential Information to any third parties for three (3) years following the date of its disclo sure by Disclosing Part y to Receiv ing Party, except to Receiving Party's consultants as set out below. However, Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information in accordance with judicial or other governmental order, and no prior notice is required to the disclosing party before such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the requirement for the Receiving Party to provide reasonable notice to the Disclosing Party before disclosing Confidential Information under judicial or governmental order. This contradicts the original clause that stipulates prior notice, creating uncertainty about the Receiving Party's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall not disclose any Conf idential Information to any third parties for three (3) years following the date of its disclo sure by Disclosing Part y to Receiv ing Party, except to Receiving Party's consultants as set out below. However, Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information in accordance with judicial or other governmental order, and no prior notice is required to the disclosing party before such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the Receiving Party to give reasonable notice to the Disclosing Party before disclosing Confidential Information under judicial or governmental order. The changed text removes this requirement, stating 'no prior notice is required'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. Therefore, the change directly contradicts the original requirement, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it removes an obligation that was previously explicitly stated. The explanation provided confirms this contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own Confidential Information, to keep confidential the Confidential Information. Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information or Confidential Materials only to Receiving Part y's employees or bona-fidae consultants on a need-to-know basis. Receiving Party will have executed or sh all execute appropriate written agreements with its employees and consultants sufficient to enable them to comp ly with all the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall take security precautions it deems necessary, to keep confidential the Confidential Information. Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information or Confidential Materials to anyone it deems necessary. Receiving Party isn't required to have any agreements with anyone.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the 'reasonable security precautions' clause and the 'need-to-know' basis for disclosing confidential information, and removing any requirement for written agreements. This contradicts the original clause's intent to safeguard confidential information, creating uncertainty and contradicting the original definition of needed precaution.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall take security precautions it deems necessary, to keep confidential the Confidential Information. Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information or Confidential Materials to anyone it deems necessary. Receiving Party isn't required to have any agreements with anyone.
perturbation.justification: The changed text fundamentally alters the obligations of the Receiving Party regarding confidential information. The original text mandates 'reasonable security precautions' and disclosure only on a 'need-to-know basis' with written agreements. The changed text removes these safeguards, allowing the Receiving Party to use precautions it 'deems necessary' (which could be none), disclose information to 'anyone it deems necessary,' and removes the requirement for any agreements. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted text are the same, and the original text is effectively contradicting the current text, the change creates a direct contradiction in the agreement regarding the level of protection and control over confidential information. Therefore, this constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as defined by inconsistencies in the definitions.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party agrees to segregate all such Confidential Materials from the confidential materials of others in order to prevent the danger of accidental disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party is not required to segregate Confidential Materials.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating the Receiving Party is not required to segregate Confidential Materials, directly contradicting the original clause that mandates segregation to prevent accidental disclosure. This creates uncertainty regarding the security measures required of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party may not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble any software disclosed to Receiving Party. Receiving Party is not required to segregate Confidential Materials.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text states 'Receiving Party is not required to segregate Confidential Materials.' The original text required segregation to prevent accidental disclosure. This creates a direct contradiction within the document because the changed text explicitly removes a previously mandated requirement. The Contradicted Text also contains the new change, solidifying the contradiction's presence.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages may not be a sufficient remedy for unauthorised disclosure of Confidential Informat ion and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies , to such compensation as may be deemed proper by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages will be the only remedy for unauthorised disclosure of Confidential Informat ion.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that monetary damages will be the only remedy. This directly contradicts the original text acknowledging that monetary damages may not be sufficient and allowing Disclosing Party to be entitled to such compensation deemed proper by a court. The enforcement is now uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party acknowledges that monetary damages will be the only remedy for unauthorised disclosure of Confidential Informat ion and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies , to such compensation as may be deemed proper by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that monetary damages will be the only remedy for unauthorized disclosure, which directly contradicts the contradicted text that acknowledges monetary damages may not be sufficient and allows for additional compensation deemed proper by a court. The original text allows for other remedies while the changed text limits the remedies, causing a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by a written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and si gned by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It can be modified with the consent of either party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating the agreement can be modified with the consent of either party. This directly contradicts the original text which says it can only be modified by a written agreement signed by both parties, creating uncertainty around the requirements for modifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It can be modified with the consent of either party. None of the provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed to have been waived by any act or acquiescence on the part of the Disclosing Party, its agents, or em ployees, but only by an instrument in writing signed by an authorised officer of Disclosing Party. No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any othe r provision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement can be modified with the consent of either party, which directly contradicts the original text stating it can only be modified by a written agreement signed by both parties. The 'Contradicted Text' is exactly the same as the Changed Text, which further emphasizes that there is a direct contradiction now in the document due to this change. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.
